s97 forensics NAMES the p79 nrefs-delta driver: RTL insns 34 (subsi3 SYMBOL_REF-diff) + 38 (addsi3 tbl+delta) emitted by expr.c::expand_expr for h5's cross-symbol source spelling; unfoldable in cse.c:

frontier.hypothesis: F97a - a semantic-purpose C form emitting >=2 additional pre-block=3 references to tbl_125c (p79) without cross-symbol arithmetic could preserve masked=2 priority basin. Un-run because no natural semantic use of tbl_125c exists at prologue in the target's observable behavior spec.
frontier.mechanism: Any such spelling would need to raise flow.c REG_N_REFS[p79] to >=5 via insns that survive cse.c fold_rtx as p79-referencing (i.e., not folded into const:SI). Candidates require a variable operand (non-const_int) upstream. Real observable uses of tbl_125c in the function are ONLY the block=3 arg4 and arg5 reads; any pre-block=3 dead read (e.g. `(void)tbl_125c[0];`) fails the human-programmer test per no-new-park-categories cheats-by-any-spelling vetting checklist.
frontier.next_probe: Structural session: enumerate honest semantic-purpose spellings that pre-touch tbl_125c[N] (e.g., early bound-checking; but the C spec has no such check). Predicted enumeration size: 0 candidates satisfying human-programmer test. If 0 candidates, F97a formally CLOSED and combined with F97b closes the entire honest-p79-uplift axis.

frontier.hypothesis: F97b - pointer-alias-fake-exception fn-scope `s32 *tbl_alias = tbl_125c;` (2026-07-01 sanction) applied to duplicate p79 references. Predicted KILL: the alias creates a NEW pseudo, splitting refs across two pseudos rather than lifting nrefs on p79.
frontier.mechanism: s89/s90 established that any spelling reducing to `SYMBOL_REF + int` at fold time collapses to probe1's 3-ref profile. A fn-scope alias `s32 *tbl_alias = D_800A125C;` emits SET of a fresh pseudo p_alias carrying REG_EQUAL (symbol_ref) and each downstream `tbl_alias[K]` reads p_alias, not p79. Total p79 refs drop to 1 (SET only, then p_alias forwards); tbl_alias absorbs the block=3 arg reads. Predicted flow-count: p79=1, p_alias=3-5.
frontier.next_probe: Structural session: measure the alias probe once via sandbox + gccdbg to formally close F97b (predicted masked>=15 based on the ref-splitting mechanism). Requires layer-2 cheat-reviewer per sanctioned-family protocol (annotate `/* FAKE: pointer-alias-fake-exception per 2026-07-01 sanction */`).

frontier.hypothesis: F97c - attack the non-nrefs priority inputs (livelen, size) rather than nrefs. Livelen is measured as luid(last_ref) - luid(first_ref) via flow.c mark_regs_live_at_end; can be perturbed by insn re-timing. Un-attacked as of s96 which pointed to this axis but did not probe.
frontier.mechanism: priority = floor_log2(refs) * refs * size / livelen * 10000. If nrefs is locked at 3 (probe1 profile) but livelen can be shortened from 148 to <60, pri could re-enter the >675 range without needing extra refs. p79's livelen 148 spans from insn 24 (prologue SET) to insn ~118 (last block=3 use). Shortening requires MOVING the initial SET closer to block=3 - but SET position is determined by C-source declaration order + expr.c expand_expr; the initializer is at src line 404 which cse.c cannot hoist across the intervening D_800F19B8/D_800F19BC stores.
frontier.next_probe: Forensics session: instrument flow.c::mark_regs_live_at_end to log luid-per-pseudo per basic block on h5 vs probe1; identify whether p79's livelen shrinks under (a) moving the tbl_125c assignment CLOSER to block=3, (b) removing D_800F19BC/D_800F19C0 stores between insn 24 and block=3, or (c) any pseudo-livespan interaction with the loop's back-edge that flow's basic-block-scanning could shortcut.

judge_constraints: Do not resurface canonical-asm authorization for cpu_side_move_dir_4 or its twins under any framing of 'exhaustion / mechanism-explained / cluster-shared' evidence; that evidence class is settled-FAIL. Next session must SHIFT MODALITY to one of the un-run axes above (SOTN-family carve-out application under codified prerequisites + FAKE annotation + layer-2 cheat-reviewer, OR ALLOCDBG-instrumented s-reg coupling hunt per register-alloc-pure-c). Report the specific carve-out or ALLOCDBG frontier chosen, not another sweep of the closed space.
